region_of_origin: Singapore (served at The Alley, a Taiwanese-origin bubble tea chain)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'yogurt drink' class (FOODON_03301710); no specific mango yogurt drink entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers fermented milk drinks including yogurt drinks. No specific mango-flavoured yogurt drink code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226760005 is the closest available concept for yogurt as a substance; no specific yogurt drink or mango yogurt drink concept exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium-sized cup of mango yogurt drink, approximately 400 ml, as typically served at The Alley bubble tea outlets in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Yogurt, Greek, plain, whole milk (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 01256) and Mangos, raw (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 09176), combined in estimated 60:40 yogurt-to-mango ratio with added sugar adjustment
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for mango yogurt drink exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Values estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for whole-milk Greek yogurt (FDC ID 1097564) and raw mango (FDC ID 169910) in an approximate 60:40 blend ratio, with an estimated 8 g/100 g added sugar to reflect commercial sweetening. Energy recalculated using Atwater factors. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — yogurt, low-fat, fruit, average GI 33; mango, GI 51; blended estimate weighted 60:40
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a commercial mango Greek yogurt drink. GI estimated as weighted average: Greek/plain yogurt GI ~20 (University of Sydney GI Database, tested value for plain whole-milk yogurt) weighted 60% and fresh mango GI ~51 (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) weighted 40%, yielding an estimated GI of approximately 32–36. A value of 36 is used as a conservative upper estimate. GL per serving calculated as (36 × 58 g available carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = 20.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~38 mg/100 g; ~152 mg per medium serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 152 mg sodium per medium serving, this drink poses a low sodium burden and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though the added sugar content should be monitored as part of overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~20.9 per medium serving) driven by added sugar and mango fructose
type2_diabetes.guidance: The estimated glycemic load of approximately 20.9 per medium 400 ml serving is moderate; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size or choose unsweetened versions, as the combined sugar from mango and added sweetener (~52 g sugar per serving) can cause meaningful postprandial glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.8 g/100 g) and beneficial micronutrients from mango (vitamin C, potassium)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The relatively low saturated fat content and the presence of potassium (~700 mg per serving) and vitamin C (~44 mg per serving) from mango make this drink a moderate cardiovascular choice, though the added sugar content (~52 g per serving) warrants caution for individuals with metabolic cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mango Yogurt Drink (Medium) is a chilled blended beverage made from fresh or puréed mango fruit combined with Greek yogurt, producing a thick, creamy, tangy-sweet drink. It is sold in medium cup size (approximately 400 ml) at The Alley, a Taiwanese-origin specialty beverage chain with outlets in Singapore, including Funan Mall. The drink is categorised under the Chinese cuisine tag in the field survey data platform, reflecting the chain's East Asian branding. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 95 kcal per 100 g, with 4.2 g protein from the Greek yogurt base, 2.8 g fat (predominantly from milk fat), and 14.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g, of which approximately 13 g is sugar from mango and added sweetener. A full medium serving of 400 g delivers roughly 380 kcal, 16.8 g protein, 11.2 g fat, and 58 g carbohydrate. The Greek yogurt component contributes calcium (~440 mg per serving), vitamin B12, and probiotics. The mango component contributes vitamin C (~44 mg per serving), vitamin A (~112 mcg RAE per serving), potassium (~700 mg per serving), and dietary fibre (~2 g per serving). The estimated glycemic index is approximately 36 (low GI), but the glycemic load per medium serving is moderate (~20.9) due to the large volume. Clinically, this drink is low in sodium and saturated fat, making it broadly suitable for cardiovascular and hypertension management, but the moderate glycemic load and high total sugar per serving require portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. It is vegetarian, gluten-free, and contains dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in The Alley mango yogurt?
Is mango yogurt drink suitable for diabetics?

Is mango yogurt drink high in protein?
Can I drink mango yogurt if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of mango yogurt drink?
Is The Alley mango yogurt gluten-free?
How much calcium is in mango yogurt drink?
What are the ingredients in mango yogurt drink?

confidence_reason: No direct entry for mango yogurt drink exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or any primary regional food composition database. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for whole-milk Greek yogurt and raw mango in an approximate 60:40 ratio with added sugar adjustment. GI is estimated as a weighted average of approved GI database values for plain yogurt and fresh mango. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data (Greek yogurt FDC 1097564 and raw mango FDC 169910) blended in an approximate 60:40 ratio with ~8 g/100 g added sugar; these are not measured values for this specific commercial product. 2. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate for a medium cup; actual volume may vary by outlet. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this estimation. 4. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in whole-milk dairy and fresh mango per USDA SR Legacy data. 6. GI estimated as weighted average of plain whole-milk yogurt (GI ~20, University of Sydney GI Database) and fresh mango (GI ~51, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); gi_category is low. 7. The food is tagged as Chinese cuisine on field survey data due to the chain's East Asian branding; the drink itself is a modern commercial beverage with no specific regional culinary tradition. 8. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore.
